body:
Follow-up to #90 (closed). Installed the published CLIs and re-ran the reference test document from that issue:

- `npm install -g @link-assistant/web-capture` → `1.7.14`
- `cargo install web-capture --force` → `0.3.6`

## TL;DR

- `--capture browser` in both CLIs produces **byte-identical** markdown that matches the reference almost perfectly — this path is in great shape.
- `--capture api` (public-export) in both CLIs produces **visibly degraded** markdown vs the reference, in different ways per language.
- Image extraction is correct in all four runs.

We'd like `--capture api` brought as close to `--capture browser` fidelity as the public-export HTML pipeline allows. See "Ask" at the bottom.

## Repro

```
URL="https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit"
web-capture "$URL" --archive zip --capture api     -o api.zip
web-capture "$URL" --archive zip --capture browser -o browser.zip
```

Reference: `docs/case-studies/issue-90/reference/markdown-test-document.md`.

## `--capture browser` — reference fidelity ✅

Rust and JS browser outputs are `diff`-identical. Every section, list, table, nested formatting, strikethrough, blockquote, and image in the reference is preserved. Only cosmetic deltas vs the reference (hr rendered as `---` not 72 dashes, nested-list indent 4 spaces vs 2, unpadded table cells) — all semantically equivalent.

## JS `--capture api` defects

- Escaped dots in headings: `## 1\. Headings` (also `2\.`, `3\.` …)
- Single-tilde strikethrough: `~Strikethrough~` instead of `~~Strikethrough~~`
- Tables mangled — each cell on its own line with blank paragraphs:
  ```
  | 
  Feature

   | 

  Supported
  …
  ```
- Blockquote paragraphs split into separate blockquotes instead of one multi-paragraph quote:
  ```
  > This is a single-level blockquote…

  > This is a multi-paragraph blockquote.

  > This is the second paragraph inside the same blockquote.
  ```
- Nested list indentation flattened — children dedent to top level.

## Rust `--capture api` defects (different set)

- Setext-style headings (`==========`, `----------`) instead of ATX.
- `*` bullets instead of `-`.
- Nested list indentation flattened.
- Blockquote paragraphs wrapped with extra blank `>` lines:
  ```
  >
  >
  > This is a single-level blockquote…
  >
  >
  ```
- Italic markers leak into heading text: `#### *Heading level 4* ####`.
- Tables use padded columns but with italic leakage in some cells.

## Images ✅ (all four runs)

Four PNGs extracted with correct dimensions (200×100, 300×150, 150×150, 150×150). 1-byte differences vs local reference are expected — Google Docs re-encodes on export.

## Ask

1. **Bring `--capture api` as close to `--capture browser` fidelity as possible.** Route the public-export HTML through the same block / list / table / blockquote / inline renderer used by the editor-model path, or add a normalization pass that fixes the defects above (`\.`/`\!`/`\(` escapes, single vs double tildes, merged blockquote paragraphs, nested-list indent preservation, ATX headings, `-` bullets, italic marker leakage in headings). The existing `gdocs_public_doc::live` tests in both CLIs already normalize these escapes before asserting — the normalizer could move into the capture pipeline itself.

2. **Keep the CI coverage and tighten it as fidelity improves.** Live tests already run in CI (`GDOCS_INTEGRATION=true`) — see [`.github/workflows/js.yml` L201-205](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/blob/main/.github/workflows/js.yml#L201-L205) and [`.github/workflows/rust.yml` L172-177](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/blob/main/.github/workflows/rust.yml#L172-L177). As defects are fixed, please remove the matching `normalizeEscapes` / `replace("\\.", ".")` calls in `js/tests/integration/gdocs-public-doc.test.js` and `rust/tests/integration/gdocs_public_doc.rs` so the assertions lock in the improvements and prevent regressions. Ideally add explicit `--capture api` parity asserts alongside the existing `--capture browser` ones (same feature checklist, same doc, both capture modes).

## Environment

- **JS:** `@link-assistant/web-capture@1.7.14` on Node 20.19.4 (note: package declares `node >=22 <23` — but the CLI ran and the issue reproduces on supported Node as well per the case-study).
- **Rust:** `web-capture 0.3.6`, stable toolchain, macOS 24.6.0.

comments.body:
We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

title: --capture api (public-export) produces degraded markdown vs --capture browser in both CLIs (v1.7.14 / v0.3.6)
